column9: 1) speedometer isn’t always accurate. Often 10-20 mph off. Not really an issue but figured I’d mention it. One trip a few weeks back, it clocked me biking at “70mph” hahahahaha
column10:
1)I’d like to see a real time active trip display. 
2)I’d like a manual trip start and end option for whenever trips don’t seem to record. Had a bunch of trips that didn’t record, both car, walking, and biking.
column18:
Cell phone GPS type carrier would be nice. 

Not to be that guy, I do wish the bikes chosen had shocks as roads in Colorado are well... you know haha. But it’s a free ebike! Who could complain! Not having shocks is the only “issue” I’ve had so far.
column23: The way it pulls you up hills with zero extra physical input from the user is very, very cool! It’s freakish feeling haha.
column24:
My experience riding an ebike has been fun. The bike is a blast to ride and such a better economical and environmentally conscious choice. 
Plenty of compliments from avid bikes on my bike and it’s features! Bike enthusiasts like it as well!
Currently not able to bike all the way home and to work as it’s very far even for this ebike, so that’s the only “bad” part. 
Well and big bumps are very sketchy. At the right speed and when turning, a big enough bump could definitely throw you off.

column23:
Short commutes are just as fast on an ebike as they are in a car, if not faster! 
I had my girlfriend drive to the grocery store while I biked this week to test this and I beat her to the store. 
Short 0.1-2 mile trips can seemingly almost be done faster on ebikes, of course depending on the route, cross streets, location, etc.

column25:
Heads up, I will be leaving town for the rest of December for Christmas vacation  back home in Alabama :)
So this is going to be my last survey for the year as I’ll be 1200 miles away from my bike the rest of this month. 
I’ll be back December 31st/January 1st and will continue to give feedback after I return home to my ebike and Colorado! I will give feedback well past the cut off date!
